nodes.title: Foundation Yr Practicum
nodes.description: Supervised practicum-based learning of 15-20 hours per week. Students are placed in human service agencies and organizations and learn the purposeful application of generalist social work theory, ethics and skills. Prerequisite: Master of Social Work Foundation year student or Coordinator of Practicum Education permission. Co-requisites: SWSS 6120; SWSS 6130.
nodes.prereq_expression: {"type": "OR", "operands": [{"type": "course", "code": "SWSS 6000"}, {"type": "course", "code": "Master of Social Work Foundation year student"}]}
nodes.other_requirements: Coordinator of Practicum Education permission

nodes.title: Iss & Pol in Social Welfare I
nodes.description: An introduction to economic, political, historical, and social forces that influence the development and implementation of social welfare policy. Prerequisite: SWSS 1020.
nodes.other_requirements: permission and prereq override; SWSS majors only

nodes.title: SL:Theory &Integration PrepSem
nodes.description: This course is a bridge between theories studied in pre- and co-requisite courses and senior year. It prepares the student for their field practicum. Prerequisites: SWSS 1020, SWSS 3470, SWSS 3670, SWSS 3650. Co-requisites: SWSS 3480, SWSS 3660. Pre/Co-requisites: SWSS 1040, SWSS 1600.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior or Senior SWSS students only, or program permission.

nodes.title: Social Work Practice II
nodes.description: Social work theory and practice methods employed by social workers in providing services to groups, organizations, and communities. Prerequisites: SWSS 4680. Co-requisites: SWSS 4720, SWSS 4740.
nodes.other_requirements: SWSS majors only

nodes.title: Iss & Pol in Social Welfare II
nodes.description: In-depth examination of social welfare policy and accompanying social services in the U.S.; major policy analysis models presented and used. Prerequisite: SWSS 1020, SWSS 3650. Co-requisites: SWSS 3480, SWSS 3630.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior or Senior SWSS students only, or program permission

nodes.title: The Found ofHum Beh&Soc Envr I
nodes.description: Invites students to use theory as a critical thinking tool for social work practice. Students will learn to interpret and apply social constructionism as a practice framework. In doing so, students will gain knowledge about discursive realities that create both norms and differences. Through making visible norms and differences as co-constructions, this class equips social work students with knowledge that is imperative to serving vulnerable populations. Prerequisite: Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission. MSW standing or instructor permission only

nodes.title: Practicum Exp Seminar II
nodes.description: Weekly integrative seminar; discussion of practice within practicum site agency. Prerequisites: SWSS 4710. Co-requisites: SWSS 4690, SWSS 4740.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior SWSS majors only

nodes.title: Theories in Social Work II
nodes.description: Provides a critical examination of traditional and contemporary theories of social work and human behavior and their application in generalist group and macro practice contexts. The second part of a two-course sequence. Prerequisites: SWSS 1020, SWSS 3470, SWSS 3650, SWSS 3670; Social Work major or Instructor permission. Co-requisites: SWSS 3630, SWSS 3660.
nodes.other_requirements: Social Work major or Instructor permission. Junior or Senior SWSS students only, or program permission

nodes.title: Critical Reflections
nodes.description: Students will learn how to critically reflect on their own experience in order to gain a deeper and more detailed understanding of themselves and their identity, particularly in a context of relating to people from varied social backgrounds which may appear to be different, or similar, to their own. Prerequisite: Foundation year Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Foundation year Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission. MSW Standing or Instructor Permission

nodes.title: Foundations of Social Work
nodes.description: An introduction to the profession of social work, its functions, values, knowledge, and the problems it addresses. Methods include: speakers from human service organizations, case examples, a field trip and unique final exam using a community experience and reflection.
nodes.other_requirements: Degree students only

nodes.title: Social Work Practice I
nodes.description: Social work theory and practice methods employed by social workers in providing services to individuals, families, and small groups. Prerequisite: Senior Standing. Co-requisites: SWSS 4710, SWSS 4730.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior Standing. SWSS majors only.

nodes.title: Foundations of SW Research
nodes.description: An introduction to qualitative and quantitative methods of applied social research including program evaluation and the evaluation of practice with additional focus on interpreting research for social work context. Prerequisite: Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission. MSW students only, or program permission

nodes.title: Integrative Appr Transform SW
nodes.description: In this course students will synthesize their exploration of their area of focus in transformative social work through scholarly reading, research and classroom presentations. Prerequisite: Completion of Foundation Year.
nodes.other_requirements: Completion of Foundation Year. MSW SPMSW year students only

nodes.title: Transformative Social Work I
nodes.description: Focuses on developing relational, profound, and generative meanings for change across populations, fields of practice and social issues. Prerequisite: Completion of Foundation Year.
nodes.other_requirements: Completion of Foundation Year; MSW Concentration Year status only; Corequisite: SWSS 6890

nodes.title: Constructing Global Social Wor
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: SPMSW Students Only.

nodes.title: Soc Welfare Pol & Services II
nodes.description: Focus is on the analysis of the economic, political, and social forces that influence the development and implementation of social welfare policy. Prerequisite: SWSS 5200. Pre/Co-requisite: SWSS 5200.
nodes.other_requirements: MSW students only, or program permission

nodes.title: Practicum Experience I
nodes.description: Supervised practicum-based learning of 15-20 hours per week. Students are placed in human service agencies and organizations and learn the application of social work, theory, ethics and skills. Prerequisite: Senior standing. Co-requisites: SWSS 4680, SWSS 4710.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing. SWSS majors only - Instructor Permission Required

nodes.title: Addressing Substance Abuse SW
nodes.description: Focuses on exploring the nature, etiology, and treatment of substance use in relation to social work practice. Set within a critical social work perspective, students critically analyze the discursive constructions related to substance use and the intersection of substance use with racism and other forms of oppression. Prerequisites: Social Work student, Specialized practice year status (completion of foundation year or having advanced student status in the Master of Social Work program).
nodes.other_requirements: completion of foundation year or having advanced student status in the Master of Social Work program). MSW SPMSW status ONLY

nodes.title: Intro Social Work Research
nodes.description: Introduction to models and methods of social research from a social work perspective. Prerequisite: SWSS 1020.
nodes.other_requirements: Permission and prereq override; SWSS majors only

nodes.title: SL:Working with Refugees
nodes.description: Provides students an interdisciplinary, entry-level opportunity to learn about the social construction of refugees, the experiences and circumstances of people who become refugees and the apparatus set up to support them using social work/social justice approach.
nodes.other_requirements: SWSS majors only or instructor permission. PACE students by permission and override.

nodes.title: De/Constructing Child Protect
nodes.description: Examines causes, effects, and responses to child abuse and neglect from historical, sociopolitical, legal, psychological and cultural perspectives.  Child abuse and neglect will be examined as a social construct and in the context of a social and racial justice and human rights perspective. Examines "taken for granted" language and assumptions that maintain power within the social construct. Prerequisite: Matriculation in the foundation year of graduate study in Social Work or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Matriculation in the foundation year of graduate study in Social Work or Instructor permission. MSW standing or instructor permission only

nodes.title: Social Work Practice I
nodes.description: A comprehensive introduction to concepts and skills employed by social workers in interactions and interventions with individuals, families, and groups is provided. Prerequisite: Master of Social Work student.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Social Work student. Foundation year MSW students only

nodes.title: Trans. Change in SW Orgs
nodes.description: This course prepares social workers to lead transformative organizational change; change that reorients the way the organization functions through critical questioning of standing organizational discourse and practices. Topics include: futures research, change models, constructivist approaches, & social inequity and difference. Prerequisite: Completion of first year of the Master of Social Work program or advanced student status in the Master of Social Work program.
nodes.other_requirements: Completion of first year of the Master of Social Work program or advanced student status in the Master of Social Work program.

nodes.title: Spec Practice Yr Practicum
nodes.description: Supervised practicum-based learning of 15-20 hours per week. Students are placed in human service agencies and organizations and apply advanced social work practice related to an area of specialized practice. Prerequisites: SWSS 5200, SWSS 5210, SWSS 5160, SWSS 5170, SWSS 5027, SWSS 6120, SWSS 6130, SWSS 6000. Co-requisite: SWSS 6140.
nodes.other_requirements: MSW standing only

nodes.title: Practicum Experience II
nodes.description: Supervised practicum-based learning of 15-20 hours per week. Students are placed in human service agencies and organizations and learn the application of social work, theory, ethics and skills. Prerequisites: SWSS 4730. Co-requisites: SWSS 4690, SWSS 4720.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor Permission ONLY

nodes.title: Practicum Exp Seminar I
nodes.description: Weekly integrative seminar; discussion of practice within practicum site agency. Co-requisites: SWSS 4680, SWSS 4730.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior SWSS majors only. This course builds upon all SWSS prerequisite courses listed above.

nodes.title: Transformative Social Work II
nodes.description: Focuses on practical applications across populations, area of practice and social issues. Prerequisites: SWSS 5160, SWSS 5170, SWSS 5200, SWSS 5210, SWSS 6120, SWSS 6130, SWSS 5027, SWSS 6000 (fall and spring), one SWSS 5000-level elective, SWSS 6140. Pre/Co-requisite: SWSS 6140.
nodes.other_requirements: one SWSS 5000-level elective, SWSS 6000 (fall and spring), MSW SPMSW year students only. Pre/Co-requisite: SWSS 6140.

nodes.title: Theories in Social Work I
nodes.description: The first of a two-course social work theory sequence designed to provide a basic conceptual framework for organizing and critiquing knowledge about human behavior in the social environment. Students will explore and critically analyze theories and perspectives that can be used to understand human experiences in the context of social work practice. Prerequisites: SWSS 1020; Social Work major with minimum Junior standing or Instructor permission. Co-requisites: SWSS 3650, SWSS 3670.
nodes.other_requirements: Social Work major with minimum Junior standing or Instructor permission. SWSS majors only. A liberal arts background is an assumed prerequisite.

nodes.title: Ldrship for Racial Justice
nodes.description: Seminar on creating racial justice through leadership. The 7 C's of leadership development are explored: consciousness of self, congruence, commitment, collaboration, common purpose, controversy with civility, and citizenship. These concepts are applied to explorations of racism and racial justice.
nodes.other_requirements: students by permission and override

nodes.title: Th Found Hum Beh&Soc Envr II
nodes.description: The second in a two-course sequence that aims to increase understandings of critical anti-racist, intersectional feminist perspectives pertinent to social work. Prerequisite: SWSS 5160. Pre/Co-requisite: SWSS 5160.
nodes.other_requirements: MSW students only, or program permission

nodes.title: Social Work Practice II
nodes.description: Emphasizes knowledge and skills of social work practice with organizations and communities. Prerequisite: SWSS 6120. Pre/Co-requisite: SWSS 6120.
nodes.other_requirements: MSW Foundation year students only

nodes.title: Adv Social Work Research
nodes.description: An analysis of social work research from methodological and theoretical perspectives is emphasized. Students will consider application in their specialized practice area. Prerequisites: SWSS 5160, SWSS 5170, SWSS 5200, SWSS 5210, SWSS 6120, SWSS 6130, SWSS 5027, SWSS 6000 (fall and spring), one SWSS 5000-level elective. Pre/Co-requisite: SWSS 5027.
nodes.other_requirements: Advanced standing or permission only; Course meets in person.

nodes.title: TA: Theories  in Social Work I
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission required

nodes.title: Soc Welfare Pol & Services I
nodes.description: An introduction to history and philosophy of social work and social welfare and the structure of service programs is provided. Prerequisite: Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Master of Social Work student or Instructor permission. MSW standing or instructor permission only
